title: Specialized Information and Referral * Down Syndrome - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Specialized Information and Referral * Down Syndrome 1 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - Assistive Technology Issues Blindness Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Deafness Disabilities Issues Down Syndrome Mental Health Issues Older Adult/Aging Issues Immigration Issues Abused Lesbians/Gay Individuals Abused Transgender Individuals LGBTQ2+ Issues Transgender Individuals Offender/Ex-Offender Issues Native American Community AIDS/HIV Issues Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a call-in center for family members of children and youth with special needs and chronic health conditions (developmental and emotional disabilities, behavioral problems, and chronic physical illnesses) and professionals who serve them. The center specializes in providing information and resources related to health information, health care access, and special needs systems and supports. All calls are confidential. Families may also request to be placed on an email list to receive monthly correspondence about issues of importance to children and youth with special needs in Oregon. 707 SW Gaines Street Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Family members of individuals age 26 and younger with special physical, emotional, behavioral, or mental health needs, including those who are at risk or suspected of having a special need. The child/youth does not have to have a diagnosed condition. Does not require documentation to prove citizenship to receive services. Hours: Message line available 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (833) 990-9930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER
